current_model_improvement.definition: The paper algorithm can be implemented using the existing admit, route, schedule, cache and feedback operations; bounded host facts; shared/group/request state; direct plans; and already standardized mechanics.
examples: exact scoring, optimization or queue algorithm inside Wasm
host-side predictor or profiler output supplied as facts
per-client, per-group, per-target or cache-object accounting in existing state
global bounded assignment using set-oriented route or schedule plans
distributed CAS state backend without changing the policy contract

new_primitive_required.definition: Faithful behavior requires authority, atomicity, lifecycle, or enacted mechanism that cannot be represented or safely validated by the current operations and standard mechanics.
examples: autonomous timers and lease expiry callbacks
multi-phase migration with reserve, copy, commit and rollback
partial cache-object transformation or typed cache hierarchy
pause, resume and preserve/discard lifecycle requested by policy
returning a stable intermediate answer while terminating reasoning
simultaneous co-scheduling rather than atomic queue insertion
model loading, replica provisioning and autoscaling control

implementation_gap.definition: The current programming model is sufficient, but the committed policy, adapter, state loop, benchmark, or host facts do not implement the paper algorithm faithfully.
examples: wrong equation, ordering, threshold, statistic or baseline
missing state transition or feedback accounting
caller-supplied oracle facts replacing a required estimator
paper operation declared but trait method falling back
synthetic benchmark rewarding the implementation by construction

current_capabilities: dense batch admission with resource limits
capacity-safe set routing over sparse feasible edges
multi-request schedule selections and token budgets
resident/prospective cache objects, byte capacity and reclaim episodes
deduplicated feedback with request, group, cache, assignment, selection and action subjects
shared, group and request-scoped persistent policy state

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: arm, refresh and cancel a typed timer or lease; emit deduplicated expiry feedback carrying timer ID and subject
candidate_primitives.why: TTL, clock aging, periodic re-evaluation and hysteresis otherwise depend on external polling facts

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: request pause, preserve, resume or discard with enacted status and preserved-state identity
candidate_primitives.why: intercept, agent thrashing and staged reasoning policies require more than omission or cancellation

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: prepare destination, reserve resources, copy incremental state, commit or abort, and report downtime/bytes
candidate_primitives.why: request.rebalance@1 is only an intent and cannot prove live-migration semantics

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: reserve destination tier, transfer object or range, commit placement, release source, and report actual bytes/latency
candidate_primitives.why: prefetch and swap intents do not establish tier placement, copy-once or rollback

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: replace, merge, split or partially reclaim a cache object while preserving typed lineage and beneficiary ownership
candidate_primitives.why: Marconi-style SSM-only reclaim and KV merging cannot be expressed as whole-object reclaim

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: validated parent/child lineage, leaf eligibility, tier and range identity for cache objects
candidate_primitives.why: RAGCache, PEEK, HotPrefix, Marconi and Pythia depend on evolving trees rather than independent objects

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: all requests in a selection begin the same execution step or the selection is not enacted
candidate_primitives.why: atomic enqueue does not guarantee simultaneous branch or gang execution

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: terminate a reasoning request while committing a validated stable answer or aggregate result
candidate_primitives.why: Dynasor-style early completion is not equivalent to cancellation

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: load or unload a model, create or remove a replica, resize capacity and report readiness/failure
candidate_primitives.why: QLM, SLOs-Serve, GoodServe and routing systems include provisioning beyond choosing existing targets

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: validated nodes, edges, AND/OR semantics, readiness, task groups and lineage revisions
candidate_primitives.why: JSON facts can carry a DAG, but the host cannot validate graph identity, atomic groups or evolution

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: evaluate and commit a bounded combination of admit, route, schedule and cache subplans against one snapshot or enact none
candidate_primitives.why: whole-workflow planners can otherwise observe or enact inconsistent cross-operation state

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: select speculative token budget or mode, verify engine support, and report accepted/rejected tokens and latency
candidate_primitives.why: SLO-aware speculation is not expressible through ordinary schedule token budgets alone

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: prepare, acquire, release and fail a named tool/runtime resource with request or group ownership
candidate_primitives.why: agent serving papers coordinate external environments whose readiness and teardown affect scheduling

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: guarantee object residency until lease expiry or explicit revocation and report violations
candidate_primitives.why: omitting an object from one reclaim plan does not guarantee TTL or workflow residency across later native pressure

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: defer a branch without terminating its parent, retain its execution and KV state, and reconsider it at a later step
candidate_primitives.why: ordinary request cancellation or omission does not prove reversible branch-width regulation

candidate_primitives.minimal_contract: atomically transfer request ownership and state between prefill and decode roles, then persist the selected destination binding
candidate_primitives.why: disaggregated conversation serving requires exactly-once KV handoff plus durable decoder affinity
